overview: The Plain Text Filter processes text files encoded in ANSI, Unicode, UTF-8, and UTF-16, with automatic Byte-Order-Mark detection. It supports multiple extraction modes: by paragraphs (groups of lines separated by empty lines), by individual lines, or using custom regex rules. The filter also handles spliced lines (continuation lines in source code) and provides inline code detection via regex patterns.

simplifierRules.help: Define rules for simplifying the representation of inline codes within extracted text units. Simplification can merge or reduce complex inline code patterns to cleaner placeholders, making segments easier to translate.

moveLeadingAndTrailingCodesToSkeleton.help:
Inline codes that appear at the very start or end of a segment are typically not meaningful to translators. Moving them to the skeleton removes them from the translatable content, producing cleaner segments. For example, if a segment is `
Hello world
`, the boundary `
` codes would be moved out, leaving just `Hello world` for translation.
notes: Only affects codes at the **boundaries** of segments — interior codes are left in place.

mergeAdjacentCodes.help:
When multiple inline codes appear next to each other with no translatable text between them, they are combined into a single placeholder. This reduces clutter in the segment presented to the translator.

For example, `{1}{2}Text{3}` would become `{1}Text{3}` where the first placeholder represents both original codes.

processingNotes: **BOM detection**: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark, the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used automatically, overriding the default encoding setting.
**Output BOM behavior**: When the output encoding is UTF-8 and the input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. If the input was not UTF-8, no BOM is written.
**Line-break preservation**: The type of line-breaks in the output always matches the original input (CR, LF, or CRLF).

examples.title: Extract by paragraphs
examples.description: When using the `okf_plaintext_paragraphs` configuration, groups of lines separated by one or more empty lines are extracted as single text units.
examples.input:
```text
This is the first paragraph
which spans two lines.

This is the second paragraph.
```
examples.output:
```text
Text unit 1: "This is the first paragraph which spans two lines."
Text unit 2: "This is the second paragraph."
```

examples.title: Spliced lines with backslash
examples.description: Using the `okf_plaintext_spliced_backslash` configuration, lines ending with `\` are merged with the following line. The splicer character can optionally become an inline code.
examples.input:
```text
This is a long \
line of text.
```
examples.output:
```text
Text unit: "This is a long line of text."
```
